envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Three-tier governed onboarding flow specifications: company-level + department-level + user-level questions, decision tree, output mappings, connector-recommendation logic, aha-moment-selection logic, permission-recommendation logic, safe-fallback logic, governance review points. **Dandelion does not activate raw templates. Dandelion assembles governed starter envelopes for Foundation governance to authorize.**
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: When Dandelion runs a flow, treat outputs as SUGGESTIONS that compose into a governed DigitalTwinStarterProfile envelope. NEVER auto-grant permissions. NEVER auto-connect connectors. ALWAYS surface to org-admin governance review at every step that crosses a permission boundary or sensitivity threshold.
audit_expectations: ADMIN_ACTION:DANDELION_FLOW_STEP per ADR-0071 at every step transition
ADMIN_ACTION:DANDELION_OUTPUT_APPROVED per ADR-0071 at governance review points
envelope_defaults.adaptation_rules: DandelionFlowTemplate adapts only through governed Wave 4+ implementation; the flow remains suggest-only at all tiers until Foundation governance authorizes activation.
envelope_defaults.override_rules: Companies may shorten / lengthen / disable question tiers via Control Tower governance review; loosening governance review points requires Founder authorization per RULE 20.

items.name: Company / Department / User Activation Flow
items.description: Three-tier governed onboarding flow: company-level setup feeds department-level setup feeds user-level role-shape selection, producing a DigitalTwinStarterProfile and a connector-priority matrix.
items.governance_notes: Dandelion suggests the starter shape; Foundation governance authorizes what may actually run. Every tier preserves RULE 0 sovereignty: no sensitive/protected-attribute inference, no employee scoring, no manager surveillance, no unapproved write actions, read-only-first for sensitive systems, delegated authority explicit, dual-control where needed, all connector actions audited.
safe_defaults: preserve override at every governance review point
company_level_questions: What compliance requirements apply (SOC 2 / HIPAA / FedRAMP / GDPR / state-level / none yet)?
Which identity provider do you use (Okta / Microsoft Entra ID / Google Workspace Identity / other)?
Which communication tools do you use (Slack / Microsoft Teams / Gmail / Outlook / other)?
Which email/calendar suite (Google Workspace / Microsoft 365 / other)?
Which document system (Google Drive / Microsoft 365 / Notion / Confluence / Box / Dropbox / other)?
Which project/work-management system (Jira / Linear / Asana / Monday / ClickUp / Trello / Wrike / Airtable / Smartsheet / other)?
Which HRIS/ATS (Workday / BambooHR / Rippling / Gusto / ADP / Greenhouse / Lever / other)?
Which finance / expense / travel system (NetSuite / QuickBooks / Xero / Ramp / Brex / Expensify / SAP Concur / Navan / TravelPerk / other)?
Which code repo (GitHub / GitLab / Bitbucket / other)?
Which support platform (Zendesk / Intercom / Freshdesk / ServiceNow / other)?
Which security/compliance platform (Vanta / Drata / OneTrust / Secureframe / Wiz / CrowdStrike / 1Password / Bitwarden / other)?
Which systems are most sensitive (compensation / customer-data / board materials / financials / IP / regulated data)?
Which tools are NOT approved for Otzar yet?
department_level_questions: Which roles are in this department?
Which tools does the department use daily?
Which workflows recur (sprint, weekly review, monthly close, hiring cycle, etc.)?
Which approvals are required at department level?
What are the top blockers right now?
Which KPIs does the department track?
Which documents are canonical for this department?
Which data is sensitive within department scope?
Which other departments does this department depend on most?
user_level_questions: Do you have direct reports? Who?
Do you have dotted-line relationships? With whom?
Which executive(s) do you support (if applicable)?
Who do you collaborate with most?
Which tools do you use daily?
Which first workflows do you want your Twin to support?
What read / write boundaries should apply by default?
Which areas should your Twin never touch?

decision_tree.step_1_company_template_selection: Industry + size + compliance + tool stack answers → CompanyTemplate selection.
decision_tree.step_2_department_template_selection: Department + role mix + daily tools + workflows → DepartmentTemplate selection.
decision_tree.step_3_role_template_selection: Title + department + manager + delegated authority + first 'wow' → RoleTemplate selection.
decision_tree.step_4_permission_bundle_recommendation: Read/write boundary + approval boundary + delegated authority → PermissionBundle suggestions (all DISABLED_UNTIL_APPROVED for risky bundles).
decision_tree.step_5_tool_profile_recommendation: Daily tools + role-template defaults → ToolProfile enable-set (read-first).
decision_tree.step_6_workflow_template_recommendation: First workflows + role-template defaults → WorkflowTemplate enable-set.
decision_tree.step_7_connector_preset_recommendation: Tool stack + connector priority logic → ConnectorPreset available list (NOT connected until governance authorizes).
decision_tree.step_8_aha_moment_selection: First wow task + role + tools → AhaMomentPack selection.
decision_tree.step_10_governance_review: Org admin + compliance review of suggested DigitalTwinStarterProfile + risky permission bundles + connector enable list.

output_mappings.PermissionBundle: suggested via step_4 (suggestions only; never auto-grant)

items.connector_recommendation_logic: Score = w1*role_frequency_across_enterprise + w2*Dandelion_collected_demand + w3*role_templates_using_tool_count + w4*collaboration_centrality + w5*read_value + w6*write_value - w7*security_risk - w8*integration_complexity + w9*customer_demand + w10*demo_impact + w11*launch_necessity + w12*cross_department_reach + w13*approval_gated_write_feasibility. Output ranked list of ConnectorPreset references. NOT a Section 4 first-adapter authorization — output is recommendation only. Founder selects.
items.aha_moment_selection_logic: First-wow answer + RoleTemplate.aha_moment_pack default + ToolProfile availability → AhaMoment ranking. Top 1-2 selected. Risk_level + approval_requirement validated against PermissionBundle suggestions.
items.permission_recommendation_logic: RoleTemplate.permission_bundles → default; user answers on read/write + approval + never-touch areas → bundle.default_state. Risky bundles default DISABLED_UNTIL_APPROVED. Forbidden permissions never suggested.
items.safe_fallback_logic: RoleTemplate.safe_fallback_mode preserved. Without any connector → no_connector_capabilities. With each ConnectorPreset enabled → corresponding capability expansion. Never bypass forbidden_capabilities.
governance_review_points: step_4: org admin reviews permission bundle suggestions before any DISABLED_UNTIL_APPROVED bundle activates
step_7: org admin reviews ConnectorPreset enable list before any connector connects (each connector then requires its own Founder-authorized Wave 7 implementation arc)
step_10: compliance / General Counsel review of any RoleTemplate that touches privileged / regulated / sensitive scope (Board, Legal, Compliance, CFO, CHRO)
any sensitive-question answer triggers explicit org-admin review before storage
every adaptation applied via ADR-0048 governed signals logs an event
